how_much.short: Depends on what you need. Quick question — are you actively taking clients right now?
how_much.medium: Fair question. It's $3,500/mo after a free trial. But before we talk numbers — are you looking to bring on more clients right now, or is this more research?
how_much.voice_note: Hey — got your question about price. So it's $3,500 a month, but here's the thing — I don't ask for that until you've seen the work. First 10 calls are free. If I deliver, we talk pricing. If I don't, you owe nothing. But real quick — are you actually looking to take on more clients right now? That'll help me know if this even makes sense for you.

send_info.short: Happy to — what specifically would be most useful? The process, results, or pricing?
send_info.medium: For sure. Quick question so I send the right thing — is this a priority right now, or more something you're exploring for later?
send_info.voice_note: Hey — happy to send info. But honestly, most info docs just get skimmed. What would actually help you decide? Is it how I find people, what qualified looks like, or the pricing? Let me know and I'll put together something relevant instead of a generic deck.

already_have_agency.short: Got it. How's that going for you — happy with the lead quality?
already_have_agency.medium: Makes sense. Just curious — are you happy with what you're getting, or is there a gap you're trying to fill?
already_have_agency.voice_note: Hey — totally get it, you've got someone. Quick question though — are you happy with the quality of calls they're booking? I ask because a lot of coaches I talk to have someone, but the leads aren't qualified — tire-kickers, no-shows, that kind of thing. If that's not you, all good. But if it is, might be worth a quick chat to compare approaches.

no_time.short: Makes sense. Is that temporary or is acquisition not a focus right now?
no_time.medium: Totally fair — capacity is real. Is this a 'not right now' or a 'not this quarter' kind of thing?
no_time.voice_note: Hey — I hear you on time. Real question: is it that you're slammed right now but want more clients eventually? Or is acquisition genuinely not a priority? If it's timing, happy to circle back in a few weeks. If it's not a fit, no worries — just want to respect your time either way.

not_taking_clients.short: Got it. Is that permanent or just for now?
not_taking_clients.medium: Makes sense. When do you think you'll be open to new clients again? Happy to check back then.
not_taking_clients.voice_note: Hey — no problem at all. Quick question: is that a permanent thing, or more of a 'full right now' situation? If you're planning to open up spots later, I can check back when timing's better. Just let me know.

hate_sales_calls.short: What specifically about them? The awkwardness, the time, or something else?
hate_sales_calls.medium: I hear that a lot. Is it the calls themselves, or that the people on them aren't serious? Because qualified calls feel different.
hate_sales_calls.voice_note: Hey — I get it, sales calls can feel weird. But here's the thing: most bad calls are because the person wasn't qualified. They're curious, not committed. When someone's already said they want coaching, can afford it, and are ready to start — the call isn't a pitch. It's just 'here's how I work, does that fit?' Way different energy. Would it help to hear what a qualified call actually looks like?

tried_this.short: What happened? Lead quality, no-shows, or something else?
tried_this.medium: That's frustrating. What specifically didn't work — the leads themselves, or the process?
tried_this.voice_note: Hey — I hear you. A lot of coaches have been burned by this stuff. What went wrong — were the leads unqualified, no-shows, or just not converting? The reason I ask: most lead gen is just volume. I do qualification first. If someone's not ready to buy, they don't get to you. That's usually the difference. Would it help if I walked you through what my qualification looks like compared to what you had before?

handling_rules: Always acknowledge first — never jump straight to reframe
Use clarify to understand the REAL objection
Tailor your reframe to their specific answer
End with a soft CTA, not a hard push
If they're not a fit, let them go gracefully
